p_steps = min(max(total_steps // 10, 1), 20)\\n\\n    def lr_lambda(current_step: int) -> float:\\n        if current_step < warmup_steps:\\n            return float(current_step + 1) / float(max(warmup_steps, 1))\\n        progress = (current_step - warmup_steps) / float(max(total_steps - warmup_steps, 1))\\n        progress = min(max(progress, 0.0), 1.0)\\n        cosine = 0.5 * (1.0 + np.cos(np.pi * progress))\\n        return 0.1 + 0.9 * cosine\\n\\n    return optim.lr_scheduler.LambdaLR(optimizer, lr_lambda=lr_lambda)\",\"text\":\"\"}],\"primary_mode\":\"summaries\",\"rationale\":\"Use the fixed-step budget more effectively by replacing the near-static scheduler with warmup+cosine over actual training steps, while pairing AdamW with a higher LR for faster early optimization in the 256-step regime.\",\"secondary_modes\":[\"indexing\",\"topk\"]}"}}
